who.pullAccent:
Most brands break
at Series A.

paragraphs.text: I'm Tom O'Keefe. I've been building brands since 1994 — back when the job still meant ink on paper and kerning by eye.
Since then I've sat in the chair at Idealab, Microsoft, Adobe, New Relic, and Evite. At New Relic I took the company from 50 employees and a $350M valuation to a $1.4B IPO in two years.

I've put my hands on 150+ brands along the way. {{Most break at Series A. Yours won't.}} I don't write decks. I build brand systems that survive the next round, the next hire, the next pivot — the ones that don't snap when the company does.

tiers.description: Build it right before you have to rebuild. Naming, wordmark, voice, and a visual system designed to outgrow you.
The full brand system, built for what you're about to become — not what you are. Product, marketing, team-facing.
The rebrand you don't have to do. Audit, evolve, stabilize the system for hypergrowth. 12 months of creative direction included.

close.statement:
Let's build the one
that doesn't break.
